'''Garreth Carvell''' (born 21 April 1980) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]]. He played in the [[Super League]] for [[Warrington Wolves]], [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Gateshead Thunder (1999)|Gateshead Thunder]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), [[Hull FC]], and [[Castleford Tigers]], and in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] for [[Featherstone Rovers]], as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Profile at leedsrugby.dnsupdate.co.uk"/><ref name="Biography at therhinos.co.uk">{{cite web|url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/player?PlayGuid=GC682365|title=Biography at therhinos.co.uk|publisher=therhinos.co.uk|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>

==Background==
Carvell was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Carvell joined [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] from junior club Stanningley in 1996.<ref>{{cite book |editor-last1=Fletcher |editor-first1=Raymond |editor-last2=Howes |editor-first2=David |title=Rothmans Rugby League Yearbook 1997 |date=1997 |publisher=Headline Publishing Group |location=London |isbn=978-0-7472-7764-4 |page=88}}</ref> He made his [[Super League]] début as a 17-year-old against the [[Sheffield Eagles]] on Friday 22 August 1997.

After a successful loan spell at the newly formed [[Gateshead Thunder (1999)|Gateshead Thunder]] in 1999, he signed for [[Hull F.C.]] in 2000 where he developed into one of the top {{rlp|pr}}s in the country, helping Hull F.C. to win the [[2005 Challenge Cup#Final|2005 Challenge Cup]] Final.

Hull F.C. reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], and Carvell played as a {{rlp|pr}} in his side's 4-26 loss.<ref>{{cite news|title=St Helens 26-4 Hull FC|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/6035553.stm|access-date=18 August 2013|newspaper=[[BBC News]]|date=14 October 2006}}</ref> He also helped Hull F.C. reach the [[2008 Challenge Cup Final]].

After signing a deal that would keep Carvell at Hull F.C. for the next three years he made a controversial U-turn and made the move to [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] after a fee was paid.<ref>{{cite web |title=Carvell changes mind on Hull deal |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/7485026.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=20 March 2025 |date=2 July 2008}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Warrington complete double deal as Carvell signs on |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/warrington-complete-double-deal-as-carvell-signs-on-932021.html |website=The Independent |access-date=20 March 2025 |date=16 September 2008}}</ref> Carvell played for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[2009 Challenge Cup]] final victory over Huddersfield, [[Challenge Cup 2010#Final|2010 Challenge Cup]] Final victory over Leeds.<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6-30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012}}</ref> and the 2012 challenge cup final victory again over Leeds. He also featured in the 2012 and 2013 Grand final defeats.

Carvell played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Leeds at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington"/><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live |df=dmy }}</ref>

Carvell played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Leeds at Wembley Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref>

Carvell played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

Midway through the 2013 season [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] announced that they had signed Carvell for the 2014 and 2015 seasons by signing him on a two-year deal. Carvell featured in the pre-season game against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]. However, in the week leading up to the first [[Super League]] game the Bradford club went into administration and Carvell made the switch to former club Hull F.C. on a one-year deal.

He then moved to the [[Castleford Tigers]] before finishing his career with a brief spell at [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] making his début for Featherstone Rovers on Sunday 26 April 2015.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ex-Leeds Rhinos, Castleford Tigers and Featherstone Rovers forward Carvell calls it a day |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ex-leeds-rhinos-castleford-tigers-and-featherstone-rovers-forward-carvell-calls-it-a-day-1812589 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |access-date=20 March 2025 |date=17 September 2015}}</ref>

===International honours===
In 2000, Carvell made his international début when he represented [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]]. Garreth represented [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] in 2006 in the [[2006 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2006]] [[Rugby League Tri-Nations|Tri-Nations]] competition held in Australia. <ref>{{cite web |title=Six new GB faces for Tri-Nations |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/5376076.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=20 March 2025 |date=25 September 2006}}</ref>

Carvell was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Myler gets England call">{{cite web|url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|title=Myler gets England call|publisher=England Rugby League|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081007232803/http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|archive-date=7 October 2008|df=dmy }}</ref>

Carvell was also named in England's 24-man [[2011 Rugby League Four Nations|2011]] [[Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] squad. He appeared off the bench against New Zealand at the [[KC Stadium]], [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]], and in the final against Australia at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]].

In September 2014, Carvell was again called up to play for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]]. This was the first time that Carvell played for Wales in 14 years,<ref name="Castleford Tigers prop Garreth Carvell handed first Wales call up in 14 years">{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9484920/castleford-prop-garreth-carvell-handed-first-wales-call-up-in-14-years|title=Castleford Tigers prop Garreth Carvell handed first Wales call up in 14 years|publisher=skysports.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref> but he had to withdraw before the tournament due to an injury.<ref name="Garreth Carvell withdraws from Wales squad for European Cup">{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/9507938/garreth-carvell-withdraws-from-wales-squad-for-european-cup|title=Garreth Carvell withdraws from Wales squad for European Cup|publisher=skysports.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>

==Post-playing==
After retiring as a player, Carvell joined [[Salford Red Devils]], working as the under-19's head coach and player welfare manager.<ref>{{cite web |title=Carvell's Career Befitting Of New Salford Roles |url=https://www.salfordreddevils.com/carvells-career-befitting-of-new-salford-roles-pt-1/ |website=Salford Red Devils |access-date=20 March 2025 |date=22 December 2015}}</ref> In 2021, he was appointed as head of rugby at [[Leigh Centurions]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Garreth Carvell appointed into new role at Leigh Centurions |url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/news/19012303.garreth-carvell-appointed-new-role-leigh-centurions/ |website=Leigh Journal |access-date=20 March 2025 |date=14 January 2021}}</ref>

Carvell was also involved in re-forming a rugby league players' union, the Rugby League Players' Association, and acts as the union's lead representative.<ref>{{cite web |title=Players’ union chief says blame society, not rugby league, for increased drug use |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/apr/23/players-union-blame-rugby-league-drug-use |website=The Guardian |access-date=20 March 2025 |date=23 April 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=‘We are failing our players’: Super League under fire for fixture crush |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/aug/23/super-league-under-fire-for-fixture-crush-rugby-league-players-union |website=The Guardian |access-date=20 March 2025 |date=23 August 2022}}</ref>
